title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a teacher giving information about a school trip to Brighton.
audio_script: Woman: Good morning, everyone. I'm going to tell you about next week's school trip to Brighton. I know most of you have been there before, but this visit is connected with your geography project. The coach will leave school at [14]eight twenty[/14], so please be outside the main gate by eight ten. We cannot wait for late students because the museum has given us a fixed time for our first tour. When we arrive, we will go straight to the [15]aquarium[/15], where a guide will explain how sea animals are protected along the coast. After that, you will have half an hour to make notes. Please take a [16]pencil[/16], not just a pen, because some of the worksheets need drawing. Lunch will be in a room above the [17]café[/17]. You may bring sandwiches, but hot food will also be available there. In the afternoon we are walking along the beach to collect information about stones and shells. You will work in groups of [18]four[/18], and each group must stay with a teacher. If it rains, we will visit the town museum instead. The coach should arrive back at school at about [19]six[/19] o'clock, although heavy traffic may make us a little late. Your parents have already received a copy of this plan by email. During the trip, mobile phones should stay in your bags unless a teacher asks you to use them for taking photos. Remember that this is a school day, so the usual behaviour rules still apply. Please check the notice board before you leave, because small changes to rooms or times may be written there. If you are not sure about anything, ask a member of staff.

questions.explanation: The teacher says the coach will leave school at eight twenty.
The teacher says they will go straight to the aquarium.
The teacher asks students to take a pencil.
The teacher says lunch will be in a room above the café.
The teacher says students will work in groups of four.
The teacher says the coach should arrive back at about six o'clock.
